chapter_title.en: Chapter 66 – The fairness and the justice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/344)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/344)
text: فِي حَدِيثٍ لَهُ أَ لَا أُخْبِرُكُمْ بِأَشَدِّ مَا فَرَضَ اللَّهُ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ فَذَكَرَ ثَلَاثَةَ أَشْيَاءَ أَوَّلُهَا إِنْصَافُ النَّاسِ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Zurara, from Al Hassan Al Bazzaz,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> in a Hadeeth of his<sup>asws</sup>: ‘Shall I<sup>asws</sup> inform you with the most difficult of what Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Imposed upon His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures?’, and he<sup>asws</sup> mentioned three things, the first of these being: ‘Fairness with the people from yourself’.
en.sarwar: 6. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from his father from al- Nadr ibn Suwayd from Hisham ibn Salim from Zurara from al-Hassan al-Bazzaz from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “In a Hadith he has said, ‘What is the most forceful obligation from Allah upon His creatures? I must ask you.’ The Imam mentioned three things of which the first was yielding to justice for people against one’s own soul.”
